565	def get_pythonized_sample_results(
566	        sample_result_args: SampleResultArgsType) -> SampleResultType:
567	    '''This function consumes GPU-side sampler results and computes
568	    Pythonized CPU-side sampler results (GPU -> CPU sync.)
569	
570	    Single-step scheduling: this function is invoked at sampling-time
571	    for immediate Pythonization.
572	
573	    Multi-step scheduling: Pythonization is deferred until after multiple
574	    GPU-side steps have been completed.
575	
576	    Args:
577	      sample_result_args: GPU-side inputs to the Pythonization process
578	
579	    Returns:
580	      Pythonized sampler results
581	    '''
582	
583	    (
584	        sample_metadata,
585	        sampling_metadata,
586	        greedy_samples,
587	        multinomial_samples,
588	        sample_results_dict,
589	    ) = (
590	        sample_result_args.sample_metadata,
591	        sample_result_args.sampling_metadata,
592	        sample_result_args.greedy_samples,
593	        sample_result_args.multinomial_samples,
594	        sample_result_args.sample_results_dict,
595	    )
596	
597	    for sampling_type in SamplingType:
598	        if sampling_type not in sample_metadata:
599	            continue
600	        (seq_group_id, seq_groups) = sample_metadata[sampling_type]
601	        if sampling_type == SamplingType.GREEDY:
602	            sample_results = _greedy_sample(seq_groups, greedy_samples)
603	        elif sampling_type in (SamplingType.RANDOM, SamplingType.RANDOM_SEED):
604	            sample_results = _random_sample(seq_groups,
605	                                            multinomial_samples[sampling_type])
606	        sample_results_dict.update(zip(seq_group_id, sample_results))
607	
608	    return [
609	        sample_results_dict.get(i, ([], []))
610	        for i in range(len(sampling_metadata.seq_groups))
611	    ]
612	
613	
614	def _sample_with_torch(
615	    probs: torch.Tensor,
616	    logprobs: torch.Tensor,
617	    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
618	    sampling_tensors: SamplingTensors,
619	    include_gpu_probs_tensor: bool,
620	    modify_greedy_probs: bool,
621	) -> SampleReturnType:
622	    '''Torch-oriented _sample() implementation.
623	
624	    Single-step scheduling:
625	    * Perform GPU-side sampling computation
626	    * Immediately Pythonize sampling result
627	
628	    Multi-step scheduling:
629	    * Perform GPU-side sampling computation
630	    * Defer Pythonization & preserve GPU-side
631	      tensors required for Pythonization
632	    '''
633	
634	    categorized_seq_group_ids: dict[SamplingType, list[int]] = {
635	        t: []
636	        for t in SamplingType
637	    }
638	    categorized_sample_indices = sampling_metadata.categorized_sample_indices
639	    for i, seq_group in enumerate(sampling_metadata.seq_groups):
640	        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
641	        sampling_type = sampling_params.sampling_type
642	        categorized_seq_group_ids[sampling_type].append(i)
643	
644	    sample_results_dict: SampleResultsDictType = {}
645	    sample_metadata: SampleMetadataType = {}
646	    multinomial_samples: MultinomialSamplesType = {}
647	    greedy_samples: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
648	
649	    # Create output tensor for sampled token ids.
650	    if include_gpu_probs_tensor:
651	        sampled_token_ids_tensor = torch.full((logprobs.shape[0], 1),
652	                                              VLLM_INVALID_TOKEN_ID,
653	                                              dtype=torch.long,
654	                                              device=logprobs.device)
655	    else:
656	        sampled_token_ids_tensor = None
657	
658	    # Counterintiutively, having two loops here is actually faster.
659	    # The first loop can run without waiting on GPU<->CPU sync.
660	    for sampling_type in SamplingType:
661	        sample_indices = categorized_sample_indices[sampling_type]
662	        num_tokens = len(sample_indices)
663	        if num_tokens == 0:
664	            continue
665	
666	        seq_group_id = categorized_seq_group_ids[sampling_type]
667	        seq_groups = [sampling_metadata.seq_groups[i] for i in seq_group_id]
668	        sample_metadata[sampling_type] = (seq_group_id, seq_groups)
669	        long_sample_indices = sample_indices.long()
670	        if sampling_type == SamplingType.GREEDY:
671	            greedy_samples = torch.argmax(logprobs[long_sample_indices],
672	                                          dim=-1)
673	
674	            if sampled_token_ids_tensor is not None:
675	                # Store sampled tokens in output tensor.
676	                sampled_token_ids_tensor[
677	                    long_sample_indices] = greedy_samples.unsqueeze(-1)
678	
679	            if modify_greedy_probs:
680	                # If required, modify the probabilities such that sampling from
681	                # the modified distribution would always sample the argmax
682	                # token id.
683	                _modify_greedy_probs_inplace(logprobs, probs,
684	                                             long_sample_indices,
685	                                             greedy_samples)
686	
687	        elif sampling_type in (SamplingType.RANDOM, SamplingType.RANDOM_SEED):
688	            max_n_in_batch = 1
689	            for seq_group in seq_groups:
690	                if seq_group.is_prompt:
691	                    sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
692	                    max_n_in_batch = max(max_n_in_batch, sampling_params.n)
693	            seq_groups_arg = (None if sampling_type == SamplingType.RANDOM else
694	                              seq_groups)
695	
696	            if flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is not None:
697	                logger.warning("FlashInfer 0.2.3+ does not support "
698	                               "per-request generators. Falling back to "

23	        self.pin_memory = is_pin_memory_available()
24	
25	    def forward(
26	        self,
27	        logits: torch.Tensor,
28	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
29	    ) -> SamplerOutput:
30	        # NOTE(woosuk): Use the original logits (before any penalties or
31	        # temperature scaling) for the top-k logprobs.
32	        # This is different from the V0 sampler, which uses the logits that
33	        # is used for sampling (after penalties and temperature scaling).
34	        # TODO(rob): provide option for logprobs post sampling.
35	        # See https://vllm-dev.slack.com/archives/C07UUL8E61Z/p1735907856007919 # noqa: E501
36	        num_logprobs = sampling_metadata.max_num_logprobs
37	        if num_logprobs is not None:
38	            raw_logprobs = self.compute_logprobs(logits)
39	
40	        # Use float32 for the logits.
41	        logits = logits.to(torch.float32)
42	        # Apply allowed token ids.
43	        logits = self.apply_allowed_token_ids(logits, sampling_metadata)
44	        # Apply bad words exclusion.
45	        logits = self.apply_bad_words(logits, sampling_metadata)
46	
47	        # Apply logits processors which can impact greedy sampling
48	        for processor in (sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.non_argmax_invariant):
49	            logits = processor.apply(logits)
50	
51	        # Apply penalties (e.g., min_tokens, freq_penalties).
52	        logits = self.apply_penalties(logits, sampling_metadata)
53	        # Sample the next token.
54	        sampled = self.sample(logits, sampling_metadata)
55	        # Convert sampled token ids to int64 (long) type to ensure compatibility
56	        # with subsequent operations that may use these values as indices.
57	        # This conversion is necessary because FlashInfer sampling operations
58	        # return int32 (while PyTorch argmax and topk return int64).
59	        sampled = sampled.long()
60	
61	        # Gather the logprobs of the topk and sampled token (if requested).
62	        # Get logprobs and rank tensors (if requested)
63	        logprobs_tensors = None if num_logprobs is None else \
64	            self.gather_logprobs(raw_logprobs, num_logprobs, token_ids=sampled)
65	
66	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
67	        sampled = sampled.to(torch.int32)
68	
69	        # These are GPU tensors.
70	        sampler_output = SamplerOutput(
71	            # The sampled tokens are expanded to 2D tensor with shape
72	            # [num_requests, 1], where each row represents one generated
73	            # token per request.
74	            sampled_token_ids=sampled.unsqueeze(-1),
75	            logprobs_tensors=logprobs_tensors,
76	        )
77	        return sampler_output
78	
79	    def apply_temperature(
80	        self,
81	        logits: torch.Tensor,
82	        temp: torch.Tensor,
83	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
84	        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
85	        return logits.div_(temp.unsqueeze(dim=1))
86	
87	    def greedy_sample(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
88	        return logits.argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
89	
90	    def sample(
91	        self,
92	        logits: torch.Tensor,
93	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
94	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
95	        """Sample logits based on sampling metadata.
96	
97	        The various logits processing functions called in this method
98	        may update the logits tensor in-place.
99	        """
100	
101	        assert not (sampling_metadata.all_greedy
102	                    and sampling_metadata.all_random)
103	        if sampling_metadata.all_random:
104	            greedy_sampled = None
105	        else:
106	            greedy_sampled = self.greedy_sample(logits)
107	            if sampling_metadata.all_greedy:
108	                return greedy_sampled
109	
110	        assert sampling_metadata.temperature is not None
111	
112	        # Apply temperature.
113	        logits = self.apply_temperature(logits, sampling_metadata.temperature)
114	
115	        # Apply logits processors that only apply to random sampling
116	        # (argmax invariant)
117	        for processor in sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.argmax_invariant:
118	            logits = processor.apply(logits)
119	
120	        # Apply top_k and/or top_p.
121	        random_sampled = self.topk_topp_sampler(
122	            logits,
123	            sampling_metadata.generators,
124	            sampling_metadata.top_k,
125	            sampling_metadata.top_p,
126	        )
127	
128	        if greedy_sampled is None:
129	            return random_sampled
130	
131	        sampled = torch.where(
132	            sampling_metadata.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS,
133	            greedy_sampled,
134	            random_sampled,
135	            out=greedy_sampled,  # Reuse tensor
136	        )
137	        return sampled
138	
139	    def compute_logprobs(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
140	        return logits.log_softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
141	
142	    def gather_logprobs(
143	        self,
144	        logprobs: torch.Tensor,
145	        num_logprobs: int,
146	        token_ids: torch.Tensor,
147	    ) -> LogprobsTensors:
148	        """
149	        Gather logprobs for topk and sampled/prompt token.
150	
151	        Args:
152	          logprobs: (num tokens) x (vocab) tensor
153	          num_logprobs: minimum number of logprobs to
154	                        retain per token
155	          token_ids: prompt tokens (if prompt logprobs)
156	                     or sampled tokens (if sampled
157	                     logprobs); 1D token ID tensor
158	                     with (num tokens) elements
159	                     Must be int64.
160	
161	        Returns:
162	          Top-k int indices tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
163	          Top-k float logprobs tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
164	          Sampled token rank tensor, (num tokens)
165	        """
166	        assert token_ids.dtype == torch.int64
167	        # Find the topK values.
168	        topk_logprobs, topk_indices = torch.topk(logprobs,
169	                                                 num_logprobs,
170	                                                 dim=-1)
171	
172	        # Get with the logprob of the prompt or sampled token.
173	        token_ids = token_ids.unsqueeze(-1)
174	        token_logprobs = logprobs.gather(-1, token_ids)
175	
176	        # Compute the ranks of the actual token.
177	        token_ranks = (logprobs >= token_logprobs).sum(-1)
178	
179	        # Concatenate together with the topk.
180	        indices = torch.cat((token_ids, topk_indices), dim=1)
181	        logprobs = torch.cat((token_logprobs, topk_logprobs), dim=1)
182	
183	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
184	        indices = indices.to(torch.int32)
185	
186	        return LogprobsTensors(indices, logprobs, token_ranks)
187	
188	    def apply_penalties(
189	        self,
190	        logits: torch.Tensor,
191	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
192	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
193	        if not sampling_metadata.no_penalties:
194	            assert sampling_metadata.prompt_token_ids is not None
195	            logits = apply_all_penalties(
196	                logits,
197	                sampling_metadata.prompt_token_ids,
198	                sampling_metadata.presence_penalties,
199	                sampling_metadata.frequency_penalties,
200	                sampling_metadata.repetition_penalties,
201	                sampling_metadata.output_token_ids,
202	            )
203	        return logits
204	
205	    def apply_allowed_token_ids(
206	        self,
207	        logits: torch.Tensor,
208	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
209	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
210	        if sampling_metadata.allowed_token_ids_mask is not None:
211	            logits.masked_fill_(sampling_metadata.allowed_token_ids_mask,
212	                                float("-inf"))
213	        return logits
214	
215	    def apply_bad_words(
216	        self,
217	        logits: torch.Tensor,
218	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
219	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
220	        if sampling_metadata.bad_words_token_ids:
221	            apply_bad_words(
222	                logits,
223	                sampling_metadata.bad_words_token_ids,
224	                sampling_metadata.output_token_ids,
225	            )
226	        return logits

22	class TopKTopPSampler(nn.Module):
23	    """
24	    Module that performs optional top-k and top-p filtering followed by
25	    weighted random sampling of logits.
26	
27	    Implementations may update the logits tensor in-place.
28	    """
29	
30	    def __init__(self):
31	        super().__init__()
32	        if current_platform.is_cuda():
33	            if is_flashinfer_available:
34	                flashinfer_version = flashinfer.__version__
35	                if flashinfer_version < "0.2.3":
36	                    logger.warning(
37	                        "FlashInfer version >= 0.2.3 required. "
38	                        "Falling back to default sampling implementation.")
39	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
40	                elif envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False:

42	                    # sampling unless VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1 (i.e., by
43	                    # default it is unused). For backward compatibility, we set
44	                    # `VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER` as None by default and
45	                    # interpret it differently in V0 and V1 samplers: In V0,
46	                    # None means False, while in V1, None means True. This is
47	                    # why we use the condition
48	                    # `envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False` here.
49	                    logger.info("Using FlashInfer for top-p & top-k sampling.")
50	                    self.forward = self.forward_cuda
51	                else:
52	                    logger.warning(
53	                        "FlashInfer is available, but it is not enabled. "
54	                        "Falling back to the PyTorch-native implementation of "

56	                        "please set VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1.")
57	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
58	            else:
59	                logger.warning(
60	                    "FlashInfer is not available. Falling back to the PyTorch-"
61	                    "native implementation of top-p & top-k sampling. For the "
62	                    "best performance, please install FlashInfer.")
63	                self.forward = self.forward_native
64	        elif current_platform.is_tpu():

69	    def forward_native(
70	        self,
71	        logits: torch.Tensor,
72	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
73	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
74	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],

77	        PyTorch-native implementation of top-k and top-p sampling.
78	
79	        The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
80	        """
81	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p)
82	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
83	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
84	
85	    def forward_cuda(
86	        self,
87	        logits: torch.Tensor,
88	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
89	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
90	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
91	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
92	        """More optimized implementation for top-k and top-p sampling."""
93	        if k is None and p is None:
94	            # We prefer `random_sample` over `flashinfer_sample` when sorting is
95	            # not needed. This is because `random_sample` does not require
96	            # CPU-GPU synchronization while `flashinfer_sample` does.
97	            probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
98	            return random_sample(probs, generators)
99	        if generators:
100	            logger.warning("FlashInfer 0.2.3+ does not support "
101	                           "per-request generators. Falling back to "
102	                           "PyTorch-native implementation.")
103	            return self.forward_native(logits, generators, k, p)
104	        # flashinfer sampling functions expect contiguous logits.
105	        # In flex_attn/triton_attn fp32 inference, logits can be non-contiguous
106	        # because of slicing operation in logits_processor.
107	        return flashinfer_sample(logits.contiguous(), k, p, generators)
108	
109	    def forward_tpu(
110	        self,
111	        logits: torch.Tensor,
112	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
113	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
114	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
115	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
116	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(logits, k, p)
117	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
118	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
119	
120	
121	def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
122	    logits: torch.Tensor,
123	    k: torch.Tensor,
124	    p: torch.Tensor,
125	) -> torch.Tensor:
126	    """
127	    Apply top-k and top-p optimized for TPU.
128	
129	    This algorithm avoids using torch.scatter which is extremely slow on TPU.
130	    This is achieved by finding a "cut-off" element in the original logit, and
131	    after thresholding the logit using this cut-off, the remaining elements
132	    shall constitute the top-p set.
133	
134	    Note: in the case of tie (i.e. multipple cut-off elements present in the
135	    logit), all tie elements are included in the top-p set. In other words,
136	    this function does not break ties. Instead, these tie tokens have equal
137	    chance of being chosen during final sampling, so we can consider the tie
138	    being broken then.
139	    """
140	    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
141	    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
142	
143	    if k is not None:
144	        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: (batch, )
145	        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
146	        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
147	
148	        # Make sure the no top-k rows are no-op.
149	        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
150	        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
151	
152	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_k_cutoff
153	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
154	
155	    if p is not None:
156	        cumprob = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
157	        top_p_mask = cumprob <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
158	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # at least one
159	
160	        top_p_count = top_p_mask.sum(dim=-1).unsqueeze(1)
161	        top_p_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_p_count)
162	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_p_cutoff
163	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
164	
165	    return logits
166	
167	
168	def apply_top_k_top_p(
169	    logits: torch.Tensor,
170	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
171	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
172	) -> torch.Tensor:
173	    """Apply top-k and top-p masks to the logits.
174	
175	    If a top-p is used, this function will sort the logits tensor,
176	    which can be slow for large batches.
177	
178	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
179	    """
180	    if p is None:
181	        if k is None:
182	            return logits
183	
184	        # Avoid sorting vocab for top-k only case.
185	        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)
186	
187	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
188	
189	    if k is not None:
190	        # Apply top-k.
191	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: B
192	        # Get all the top_k values.
193	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
194	        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
195	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
196	
197	    if p is not None:
198	        # Apply top-p.
199	        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
200	        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)

211	def apply_top_k_only(
212	    logits: torch.Tensor,
213	    k: torch.Tensor,
214	) -> torch.Tensor:
215	    """
216	    Apply top-k mask to the logits.
217	
218	    This implementation doesn't involve sorting the entire vocab.
219	
220	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
221	    """
222	    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
223	    # Set non-top-k rows to 1 so that we can gather.
224	    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
225	    max_top_k = k.max()
226	    # topk.values tensor has shape [batch_size, max_top_k].
227	    # Convert top k to 0-based index in range [0, max_top_k).
228	    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
229	    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
230	    # Handle non-topk rows.
231	    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
232	    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
233	    return logits

235	
236	def random_sample(
237	    probs: torch.Tensor,
238	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
239	) -> torch.Tensor:
240	    """Randomly sample from the probabilities.
241	
242	    We use this function instead of torch.multinomial because torch.multinomial
243	    causes CPU-GPU synchronization.
244	    """
245	    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
246	    # NOTE(woosuk): To batch-process the requests without their own seeds,
247	    # which is the common case, we first assume that every request does
248	    # not have its own seed. Then, we overwrite the values for the requests
249	    # that have their own seeds.
250	    if len(generators) != probs.shape[0]:
251	        q.exponential_()
252	    if generators:
253	        # TODO(woosuk): This can be slow because we handle each request
254	        # one by one. Optimize this.
255	        for i, generator in generators.items():
256	            q[i].exponential_(generator=generator)
257	    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
258	
259	
260	def flashinfer_sample(
261	    logits: torch.Tensor,
262	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
263	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
264	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
265	) -> torch.Tensor:
266	    """Sample from the logits using FlashInfer.
267	
268	    Statistically, this function is equivalent to the `random_sample` function.
269	    However, this function is faster because it avoids sorting the logits tensor
270	    via rejection sampling.
271	
272	    NOTE: The outputs of this function do not necessarily match the outputs of
273	    the `random_sample` function. It only guarantees that the outputs are
274	    statistically equivalent.
275	
276	    NOTE: This function includes CPU-GPU synchronization, while `random_sample`
277	    does not. Call this function at the end of the forward pass to minimize
278	    the synchronization overhead.
279	    """
280	    assert not (k is None and p is None)
281	    if k is None:
282	        # Top-p only.
283	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
284	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(
285	            probs, p, deterministic=True)
286	    elif p is None:
287	        # Top-k only.
288	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
289	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(
290	            probs, k, deterministic=True)
291	    else:
292	        # Both top-k and top-p.
293	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(
294	            logits, k, p, deterministic=True)
295	
296	    return next_token_ids.view(-1)

## vLLM Sampling Parameters Data Flow Analysis

I've thoroughly explored the vLLM codebase to understand the sampling parameter structure. Here's a complete guide for implementing the new "Top-A" sampling parameter:

---

## 1. MAIN FILES INVOLVED

### **SamplingParams Definition**
- **File**: `/vllm/sampling_params.py`
- **Class**: `SamplingParams` (msgspec.Struct-based)

### **Data Flow Layer 1: Metadata**
- **File**: `/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`
- **Classes**:
  - `SequenceGroupToSample` (carries `SamplingParams` per sequence group)
  - `SamplingTensors` (tensor-batched parameters for GPU computation)

### **Sampler Implementation (V0 - Primary)**
- **File**: `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`
- **Class**: `Sampler`
- **Key functions**: `_apply_top_k_top_p()`, `_apply_min_p()`

### **Sampler Implementation (V1 - Alternative)**
- **File**: `/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py`
- **V1 Metadata**: `/vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py`
- **TopK TopP Ops**: `/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`

---

## 2. PARAMETER DEFINITIONS IN SamplingParams

From `/vllm/sampling_params.py` (lines 212-214):

```python
top_p: float = 1.0
top_k: int = 0
min_p: float = 0.0
```

### **In `from_optional()` factory method** (lines 259-261):

```python
top_p: Optional[float] = 1.0,
top_k: int = 0,
min_p: float = 0.0,
```

def forward(
    self,
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
) -> Optional[SamplerOutput]:
    
    # Prepare sampling tensors with pinned memory to avoid blocking.
    if not sampling_metadata.reuse_sampling_tensors:
        self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
    
    sampling_tensors = self._sampling_tensors
    do_top_p_top_k = self._do_top_p_top_k
    do_min_p = self._do_min_p
    
    # Apply penalties (presence, frequency, repetition)
    if do_penalties:
        logits = apply_penalties(logits, ...)
    
    # Temperature scaling
    logits.div_(sampling_tensors.temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    
    # Top-k and top-p filtering (if enabled)
    if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
        logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
                                    sampling_tensors.top_ks)
    
    # Min-p filtering (if enabled)
    if do_min_p:
        logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)
```

---

## 4. FILTERING LOGIC IMPLEMENTATIONS

### **min_p Filtering Logic** (lines 427-441 in sampler.py):

```python
def _apply_min_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    min_p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Adapted from
    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
    """
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))

    return logits
```

**How it works:**
1. Converts logits to probabilities
2. Finds the maximum probability per batch
3. Scales min_p by the maximum probability
4. Masks logits to -inf where probability < scaled_min_p
5. This ensures only tokens with probability ≥ (min_p × max_prob) remain

### **top_k + top_p Filtering Logic** (lines 398-424 in sampler.py):

```python
def _apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    p: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)

    # Apply top-k.
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
    # Get all the top_k values.
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Apply top-p.
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
    # at least one
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Re-sort the probabilities.
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
                                                    index=logits_idx,
                                                    src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

**How it works:**
1. Sorts logits in ascending order
2. **Top-k**: Keeps only the k highest logits, masks others to -inf
3. **Top-p**: Computes cumulative probability in sorted order, masks tokens where cumsum ≤ (1 - p)
4. Ensures at least one token remains (even if top-p threshold is never reached)
5. Re-sorts back to original vocabulary order

def __post_init__(self) -> None:
    # best_of handling
    if self.best_of:
        if self.best_of < self.n:
            raise ValueError(...)
        if not self._real_n:
            self._real_n = self.n
            self.n = self.best_of

    # Temperature warnings
    if 0 < self.temperature < _MAX_TEMP:
        logger.warning("temperature %s is less than %s...")
        self.temperature = max(self.temperature, _MAX_TEMP)

    # Seed handling
    if self.seed == -1:
        self.seed = None

    # Stop token handling
    if self.stop is None:
        self.stop = []
    elif isinstance(self.stop, str):
        self.stop = [self.stop]

    self._verify_args()

    # Greedy sampling override
    if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
        self.top_p = 1.0
        self.top_k = 0
        self.min_p = 0.0
        self._verify_greedy_sampling()
```

   def _apply_top_a(
       logits: torch.Tensor,
       top_a: torch.Tensor,
   ) -> torch.Tensor:
       """Apply Top-A filtering.
       
       Top-A: only sample from tokens that are at least (top_a * max_prob) probability.
       Different from min_p which is: only sample from tokens with (min_p * top_prob).
       """
       probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
       top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
       scaled_top_a = top_a.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs

```

**From `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` (line 16):**

```

---

## 8. KEY DIFFERENCES: min_p vs top_a

**min_p** (existing):
- Threshold: `prob ≥ min_p × max_prob`
- Keeps tokens if they're at least `min_p` fraction of the most likely token

**top_a** (to implement):
- Threshold: `prob ≥ top_a × max_prob`
- Same logic as min_p!
- The difference is semantic/conceptual, not computational

⚠️ **Note**: Top-A and min_p have identical mathematical definitions. If Top-A is meant to be different, you may need a different formula. The common Top-A definition is: "sample from tokens whose cumulative probability from max token is ≤ top_a".

| `/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | CPU-side batching & tensor preparation | `SequenceGroupToSample`, `SamplingTensors` |
| `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | GPU-side sampling | `Sampler` class, `_apply_min_p()`, `_apply_top_k_top_p()` |
| `/vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py` | V1 metadata (alternative implementation) | V1 `SamplingMetadata` |
| `/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` | V1 filtering implementations | `apply_top_k_top_p()`, `apply_top_k_only()` |

This comprehensive structure ensures that sampling parameters flow consistently from user input through GPU execution with proper batching and tensor organization.

    # Top-k and top-p filtering (if enabled)
    if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
        logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
                                    sampling_tensors.top_ks)

    if do_min_p:
        logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)

    elif isinstance(self.stop, str):
        self.stop = [self.stop]

    self._verify_args()

    # Greedy sampling override
    if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
        self.top_p = 1.0
        self.top_k = 0
        self.min_p = 0.0
        self._verify_greedy_sampling()
```

### **Decision Logic for Enabling Filters** (lines 426-434 in sampling_metadata.py):

```python
do_top_p_top_k = False
do_min_p = False

# ... in loop ...
if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
                           or top_k != vocab_size):
    do_top_p_top_k = True
if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
    do_min_p = True
```

**Key threshold**: `_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5`

## 6. IMPLEMENTATION CHECKLIST FOR "Top-A" PARAMETER

To add Top-A sampling, you need to:

### **A. Add to SamplingParams** (`vllm/sampling_params.py`)

1. **Add field declaration** (after line 214):

                        f"{self.top_a}.")
   ```

5. **Add greedy sampling override** (after line 381):

   ```

6. **Add to `__repr__`** (line 572)

### **B. Add to SamplingTensors** (`vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`)

1. **Add field to SamplingTensors dataclass** (after line 379):

   ```

2. **Add to extraction logic** (after line 416):

3. **Add to decision logic** (after line 429):

       do_top_a = True
   ```

4. **Add to accumulation logic** in both prefill and decode sections (after 446 and 457):
   ```python

   ```

5. **Add to `from_lists()` method** (line 502 parameter list):

6. **Add tensor creation** (after line 556):

   ```

   ```

8. **Update `from_sampling_metadata()` return** (line 495) to return `do_top_a`

### **C. Add Filtering Logic to Sampler** (`vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`)

1. **Add new filter function** (after line 441):
   ```python
   def _apply_top_a(
       logits: torch.Tensor,
       top_a: torch.Tensor,
   ) -> torch.Tensor:
       """Apply Top-A filtering.
       
       Top-A: only sample from tokens that are at least (top_a * max_prob) probability.
       Different from min_p which is: only sample from tokens with (min_p * top_prob).
       """
       probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
       top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
       scaled_top_a = top_a.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
       tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_top_a
       logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
       return logits
   ```

2. **Update `_init_sampling_tensors()`** (around line 217):
   - Capture `do_top_a` from `SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()` return
   - Store in `self._do_top_a`

3. **Update `forward()` method** (after line 289):

   ```

### **D. V1 Implementation** (`vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py` and related)

1. Add `top_a: Optional[torch.Tensor]` to `SamplingMetadata` dataclass
2. Update `/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` to include Top-A filtering
3. Update `/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` to call Top-A filtering

## 7. CONSTANT DEFINITIONS

**From `vllm/sampling_params.py` (lines 20-21):**
```python

_MAX_TEMP = 1e-2
```

**From `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` (line 16):**
```python
_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5
```

---

## 8. KEY DIFFERENCES: min_p vs top_a

**min_p** (existing):
- Threshold: `prob ≥ min_p × max_prob`
- Keeps tokens if they're at least `min_p` fraction of the most likely token

**top_a** (to implement):
- Threshold: `prob ≥ top_a × max_prob`
- Same logic as min_p!
- The difference is semantic/conceptual, not computational

⚠️ **Note**: Top-A and min_p have identical mathematical definitions. If Top-A is meant to be different, you may need a different formula. The common Top-A definition is: "sample from tokens whose cumulative probability from max token is ≤ top_a".

---

## 9. FILE SUMMARY

| File | Purpose | Key Components |
|------|---------|-----------------|
| `/vllm/sampling_params.py` | Parameter definition & validation | `SamplingParams` class, validation logic |
| `/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | CPU-side batching & tensor preparation | `SequenceGroupToSample`, `SamplingTensors` |
| `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | GPU-side sampling | `Sampler` class, `_apply_min_p()`, `_apply_top_k_top_p()` |
| `/vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py` | V1 metadata (alternative implementation) | V1 `SamplingMetadata` |
| `/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` | V1 filtering implementations | `apply_top_k_top_p()`, `apply_top_k_only()` |

This comprehensive structure ensures that sampling parameters flow consistently from user input through GPU execution with proper batching and tensor organization.

356	            self.stop = [self.stop]
357	
358	        if self.stop_token_ids is None:
359	            self.stop_token_ids = []
360	
361	        if self.bad_words is None:
362	            self.bad_words = []
363	
364	        if self.logprobs is True:
365	            self.logprobs = 1
366	
367	        if self.prompt_logprobs is True:
368	            self.prompt_logprobs = 1
369	
370	        # Number of characters to hold back for stop string evaluation
371	        # until sequence is finished.
372	        if self.stop and not self.include_stop_str_in_output:
373	            self.output_text_buffer_length = max(len(s) for s in self.stop) - 1
374	
375	        self._verify_args()
376	
377	        if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
378	            # Zero temperature means greedy sampling.
379	            self.top_p = 1.0
380	            self.top_k = 0
381	            self.min_p = 0.0
382	            self._verify_greedy_sampling()
383	
384	        # eos_token_id is added to this by the engine
385	        self._all_stop_token_ids.update(self.stop_token_ids)
386	
387	    def _verify_args(self) -> None:
388	        if not isinstance(self.n, int):
389	            raise ValueError(f"n must be an int, but is of "
390	                             f"type {type(self.n)}")
391	        if self.n < 1:
392	            raise ValueError(f"n must be at least 1, got {self.n}.")
393	        if self.best_of is not None:
394	            if not isinstance(self.best_of, int):
395	                raise ValueError(
396	                    f"best_of must be an integer, got {type(self.best_of)}")
397	            if self.best_of < 1:
398	                raise ValueError(
399	                    f"best_of must be at least 1, got {self.best_of}")
400	            if self.best_of < self.n:
401	                raise ValueError(
402	                    f"best_of must be greater than or equal to n, "
403	                    f"got n={self.n} and best_of={self.best_of}.")
404	        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
405	            raise ValueError("presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
406	                             f"{self.presence_penalty}.")
407	        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
408	            raise ValueError("frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
409	                             f"{self.frequency_penalty}.")
410	        if self.repetition_penalty <= 0.0:
411	            raise ValueError(
412	                "repetition_penalty must be greater than zero, got "
413	                f"{self.repetition_penalty}.")
414	        if self.temperature < 0.0:
415	            raise ValueError(
416	                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}.")
417	        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
418	            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
419	        # quietly accept -1 as disabled, but prefer 0
420	        if self.top_k < -1:
421	            raise ValueError(f"top_k must be 0 (disable), or at least 1, "
422	                             f"got {self.top_k}.")
423	        if not isinstance(self.top_k, int):
424	            raise TypeError(
425	                f"top_k must be an integer, got {type(self.top_k).__name__}")
426	        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
427	            raise ValueError("min_p must be in [0, 1], got "
428	                             f"{self.min_p}.")
429	        if self.max_tokens is not None and self.max_tokens < 1:
430	            raise ValueError(
431	                f"max_tokens must be at least 1, got {self.max_tokens}.")
432	        if self.min_tokens < 0:
433	            raise ValueError(f"min_tokens must be greater than or equal to 0, "
434	                             f"got {self.min_tokens}.")
435	        if self.max_tokens is not None and self.min_tokens > self.max_tokens:
436	            raise ValueError(
437	                f"min_tokens must be less than or equal to "
438	                f"max_tokens={self.max_tokens}, got {self.min_tokens}.")
439	        if self.logprobs is not None and self.logprobs < 0:
440	            raise ValueError(
441	                f"logprobs must be non-negative, got {self.logprobs}.")
442	        if self.prompt_logprobs is not None and self.prompt_logprobs < 0:
443	            raise ValueError(f"prompt_logprobs must be non-negative, got "
444	                             f"{self.prompt_logprobs}.")
445	        if (self.truncate_prompt_tokens is not None
446	                and self.truncate_prompt_tokens < 1):
447	            raise ValueError(f"truncate_prompt_tokens must be >= 1, "
448	                             f"got {self.truncate_prompt_tokens}")
449	        assert isinstance(self.stop_token_ids, list)
450	        if not all(isinstance(st_id, int) for st_id in self.stop_token_ids):
451	            raise ValueError(f"stop_token_ids must contain only integers, "
452	                             f"got {self.stop_token_ids}.")
453	        assert isinstance(self.stop, list)
454	        if any(not stop_str for stop_str in self.stop):
455	            raise ValueError("stop cannot contain an empty string.")
456	        if self.stop and not self.detokenize:
457	            raise ValueError(
458	                "stop strings are only supported when detokenize is True. "
459	                "Set detokenize=True to use stop.")
460	        if self.best_of != self._real_n and self.output_kind == (
461	                RequestOutputKind.DELTA):
462	            raise ValueError("best_of must equal n to use output_kind=DELTA")
463	
464	    def _verify_greedy_sampling(self) -> None:
465	        if self.n > 1:
466	            raise ValueError("n must be 1 when using greedy sampling, "
467	                             f"got {self.n}.")
468	
469	    def update_from_generation_config(
470	            self,
471	            generation_config: dict[str, Any],
472	            model_eos_token_id: Optional[int] = None) -> None:
473	        """Update if there are non-default values from generation_config"""
474	
475	        if model_eos_token_id is not None:
476	            # Add the eos token id into the sampling_params to support
477	            # min_tokens processing.
478	            self._all_stop_token_ids.add(model_eos_token_id)
479	
480	        # Update eos_token_id for generation
481	        if (eos_ids := generation_config.get("eos_token_id")) is not None:
482	            # it can be either int or list of int
483	            eos_ids = {eos_ids} if isinstance(eos_ids, int) else set(eos_ids)
484	            if model_eos_token_id is not None:
485	                # We don't need to include the primary eos_token_id in
486	                # stop_token_ids since it's handled separately for stopping
487	                # purposes.
488	                eos_ids.discard(model_eos_token_id)
489	            if eos_ids:
490	                self._all_stop_token_ids.update(eos_ids)
491	                if not self.ignore_eos:
492	                    eos_ids.update(self.stop_token_ids)
493	                    self.stop_token_ids = list(eos_ids)
494	
495	    def update_from_tokenizer(self, tokenizer: AnyTokenizer) -> None:
496	        if not self.bad_words:
497	            return
498	        self._bad_words_token_ids = []
499	        for bad_word in self.bad_words:
500	            # To prohibit words both at the beginning
501	            # and in the middle of text
502	            # (related to add_prefix_space tokenizer parameter)
503	            for add_prefix_space in [False, True]:
504	                prefix = " " if add_prefix_space else ""
505	                prompt = prefix + bad_word.lstrip()
506	                prompt_token_ids = tokenizer.encode(text=prompt,
507	                                                    add_special_tokens=False)
508	
509	                # If no space at the beginning
510	                # or if prefix space produces a new word token
511	                if (not add_prefix_space) or (
512	                        add_prefix_space and prompt_token_ids[0]
513	                        != self._bad_words_token_ids[-1][0]
514	                        and len(prompt_token_ids) == len(
515	                            self._bad_words_token_ids[-1])):
516	                    self._bad_words_token_ids.append(prompt_token_ids)
517	
518	        invalid_token_ids = [
519	            token_id for bad_words_token_ids in self._bad_words_token_ids
520	            for token_id in bad_words_token_ids
521	            if token_id < 0 or token_id > tokenizer.max_token_id
522	        ]
523	        if len(invalid_token_ids) > 0:
524	            raise ValueError(
525	                f"The model vocabulary size is {tokenizer.max_token_id+1},"
526	                f" but the following tokens"
527	                f" were specified as bad: {invalid_token_ids}."
528	                f" All token id values should be integers satisfying:"
529	                f" 0 <= token_id <= {tokenizer.max_token_id}.")
530	
531	    @cached_property
532	    def sampling_type(self) -> SamplingType:
533	        if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
534	            return SamplingType.GREEDY
535	        if self.seed is not None:
536	            return SamplingType.RANDOM_SEED
537	        return SamplingType.RANDOM
538	
539	    @property
540	    def all_stop_token_ids(self) -> set[int]:
541	        return self._all_stop_token_ids
542	
543	    @property
544	    def bad_words_token_ids(self) -> Optional[list[list[int]]]:
545	        # For internal use only. Backward compatibility not guaranteed
546	        return self._bad_words_token_ids
547	
548	    def clone(self) -> "SamplingParams":
549	        """Deep copy, but maybe not the LogitsProcessor objects.
550	
551	        LogitsProcessor objects may contain an arbitrary, nontrivial amount of
552	        data that is expensive to copy. However, if not copied, the processor
553	        needs to support parallel decoding for multiple sequences
554	        See https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/3087
555	        """
556	
557	        logit_processor_refs = None if self.logits_processors is None else {
558	            id(lp): lp.clone() if hasattr(lp, 'clone') else lp
559	            for lp in self.logits_processors
560	        }
561	        return copy.deepcopy(self, memo=logit_processor_refs)
562	
563	    def __repr__(self) -> str:
564	        return (
565	            f"SamplingParams(n={self.n}, "
566	            f"presence_penalty={self.presence_penalty}, "
567	            f"frequency_penalty={self.frequency_penalty}, "
568	            f"repetition_penalty={self.repetition_penalty}, "
569	            f"temperature={self.temperature}, "
570	            f"top_p={self.top_p}, "
571	            f"top_k={self.top_k}, "
572	            f"min_p={self.min_p}, "
573	            f"seed={self.seed}, "
574	            f"stop={self.stop}, "
575	            f"stop_token_ids={self.stop_token_ids}, "
576	            f"bad_words={self.bad_words}, "
577	            f"include_stop_str_in_output={self.include_stop_str_in_output}, "
578	            f"ignore_eos={self.ignore_eos}, "
579	            f"max_tokens={self.max_tokens}, "
580	            f"min_tokens={self.min_tokens}, "
581	            f"logprobs={self.logprobs}, "
582	            f"prompt_logprobs={self.prompt_logprobs}, "
583	            f"skip_special_tokens={self.skip_special_tokens}, "
584	            "spaces_between_special_tokens="
585	            f"{self.spaces_between_special_tokens}, "
586	            f"truncate_prompt_tokens={self.truncate_prompt_tokens}, "
587	            f"guided_decoding={self.guided_decoding}, "
588	            f"extra_args={self.extra_args})")
589	
590	
591	class BeamSearchParams(
592	        msgspec.Struct,
593	        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
594	        # required for @cached_property.
595	        dict=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
596	    """Beam search parameters for text generation."""
597	    beam_width: int
598	    max_tokens: int
599	    ignore_eos: bool = False
600	    temperature: float = 0.0
601	    length_penalty: float = 1.0
602	    include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False
603

# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright contributors to the vLLM project
"""Sampling parameters for text generation."""
import copy
from dataclasses import dataclass
from enum import Enum, IntEnum
from functools import cached_property
from typing import Annotated, Any, Optional, Union

import msgspec
from pydantic import BaseModel
from typing_extensions import deprecated

from vllm.logger import init_logger
from vllm.logits_process import LogitsProcessor
from vllm.transformers_utils.tokenizer import AnyTokenizer

logger = init_logger(__name__)

_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5
_MAX_TEMP = 1e-2


class SamplingType(IntEnum):
    GREEDY = 0
    RANDOM = 1
    RANDOM_SEED = 2


# maybe make msgspec?
@dataclass
class GuidedDecodingParams:
    """One of these fields will be used to build a logit processor."""
    json: Optional[Union[str, dict]] = None
    regex: Optional[str] = None
    choice: Optional[list[str]] = None
    grammar: Optional[str] = None
    json_object: Optional[bool] = None
    """These are other options that can be set"""
    backend: Optional[str] = None
    backend_was_auto: bool = False
    disable_fallback: bool = False
    disable_any_whitespace: bool = False
    disable_additional_properties: bool = False
    whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None
    structural_tag: Optional[str] = None

    @staticmethod
    def from_optional(
        json: Optional[Union[dict, BaseModel, str]] = None,
        regex: Optional[str] = None,
        choice: Optional[list[str]] = None,
        grammar: Optional[str] = None,
        json_object: Optional[bool] = None,
        backend: Optional[str] = None,
        whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None,
        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
    ) -> Optional["GuidedDecodingParams"]:
        if all(arg is None for arg in (json, regex, choice, grammar,
                                       json_object, structural_tag)):
            return None
        # Extract json schemas from pydantic models
        if isinstance(json, (BaseModel, type(BaseModel))):
            json = json.model_json_schema()
        return GuidedDecodingParams(
            json=json,
            regex=regex,
            choice=choice,
            grammar=grammar,
            json_object=json_object,
            backend=backend,
            whitespace_pattern=whitespace_pattern,
            structural_tag=structural_tag,
        )

    def __post_init__(self):
        """Validate that some fields are mutually exclusive."""
        guide_count = sum([
            self.json is not None, self.regex is not None, self.choice
            is not None, self.grammar is not None, self.json_object is not None
        ])
        if guide_count > 1:
            raise ValueError(
                "You can only use one kind of guided decoding but multiple are "
                f"specified: {self.__dict__}")

        if self.backend is not None and ":" in self.backend:
            self._extract_backend_options()

    @deprecated(
        "Passing guided decoding backend options inside backend in the format "
        "'backend:...' is deprecated. This will be removed in v0.10.0. Please "
        "use the dedicated arguments '--disable-fallback', "
        "'--disable-any-whitespace' and '--disable-additional-properties' "
        "instead.")
    def _extract_backend_options(self):
        """Extract backend options from the backend string."""
        assert isinstance(self.backend, str)
        self.backend, options = self.backend.split(":")
        options_set = set(options.strip().split(","))
        if "no-fallback" in options_set:
            self.disable_fallback = True
        if "disable-any-whitespace" in options_set:
            self.disable_any_whitespace = True
        if "no-additional-properties" in options_set:
            self.disable_additional_properties = True


class RequestOutputKind(Enum):
    # Return entire output so far in every RequestOutput
    CUMULATIVE = 0
    # Return only deltas in each RequestOutput
    DELTA = 1
    # Do not return intermediate RequestOutput
    FINAL_ONLY = 2


class SamplingParams(
        msgspec.Struct,
        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
        # required for @cached_property.
        dict=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
    """Sampling parameters for text generation.

    Overall, we follow the sampling parameters from the OpenAI text completion
    API (https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/create).
    In addition, we support beam search, which is not supported by OpenAI.

    Args:
        n: Number of output sequences to return for the given prompt.
        best_of: Number of output sequences that are generated from the prompt.
            From these `best_of` sequences, the top `n` sequences are returned.
            `best_of` must be greater than or equal to `n`. By default,
            `best_of` is set to `n`. Warning, this is only supported in V0.
        presence_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether they
            appear in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the model
            to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to repeat
            tokens.
        frequency_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on their
            frequency in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the
            model to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to
            repeat tokens.
        repetition_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether
            they appear in the prompt and the generated text so far. Values > 1
            encourage the model to use new tokens, while values < 1 encourage
            the model to repeat tokens.
        temperature: Float that controls the randomness of the sampling. Lower
            values make the model more deterministic, while higher values make
            the model more random. Zero means greedy sampling.
        top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens
            to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens.
        top_k: Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
            to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.
        min_p: Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be
            considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token.
            Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this.
        seed: Random seed to use for the generation.
        stop: list of strings that stop the generation when they are generated.
            The returned output will not contain the stop strings.
        stop_token_ids: list of tokens that stop the generation when they are
            generated. The returned output will contain the stop tokens unless
            the stop tokens are special tokens.
        bad_words: list of words that are not allowed to be generated.
            More precisely, only the last token of a corresponding
            token sequence is not allowed when the next generated token
            can complete the sequence.
        include_stop_str_in_output: Whether to include the stop strings in
            output text. Defaults to False.
        ignore_eos: Whether to ignore the EOS token and continue generating
            tokens after the EOS token is generated.
        max_tokens: Maximum number of tokens to generate per output sequence.
        min_tokens: Minimum number of tokens to generate per output sequence
            before EOS or stop_token_ids can be generated
        logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per output token.
            When set to None, no probability is returned. If set to a non-None
            value, the result includes the log probabilities of the specified
            number of most likely tokens, as well as the chosen tokens.
            Note that the implementation follows the OpenAI API: The API will
            always return the log probability of the sampled token, so there
            may be up to `logprobs+1` elements in the response.
        prompt_logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per prompt token.
        detokenize: Whether to detokenize the output. Defaults to True.
        skip_special_tokens: Whether to skip special tokens in the output.
        spaces_between_special_tokens: Whether to add spaces between special
            tokens in the output.  Defaults to True.
        logits_processors: list of functions that modify logits based on
            previously generated tokens, and optionally prompt tokens as
            a first argument.
        truncate_prompt_tokens: If set to -1, will use the truncation size
            supported by the model. If set to an integer k, will use only
            the last k tokens from the prompt (i.e., left truncation).
            Defaults to None (i.e., no truncation).
        guided_decoding: If provided, the engine will construct a guided
            decoding logits processor from these parameters. Defaults to None.
        logit_bias: If provided, the engine will construct a logits processor
            that applies these logit biases. Defaults to None.
        allowed_token_ids: If provided, the engine will construct a logits
            processor which only retains scores for the given token ids.
            Defaults to None.
        extra_args: Arbitrary additional args, that can be used by custom
            sampling implementations, plugins, etc. Not used by any in-tree
            sampling implementations.
    """

    n: int = 1
    best_of: Optional[int] = None
    _real_n: Optional[int] = None
    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
    repetition_penalty: float = 1.0
    temperature: float = 1.0
    top_p: float = 1.0
    top_k: int = 0
    min_p: float = 0.0
    seed: Optional[int] = None
    stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None
    stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
    ignore_eos: bool = False
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16
    min_tokens: int = 0
    logprobs: Optional[int] = None
    prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None
    # NOTE: This parameter is only exposed at the engine level for now.
    # It is not exposed in the OpenAI API server, as the OpenAI API does
    # not support returning only a list of token IDs.
    detokenize: bool = True
    skip_special_tokens: bool = True
    spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True
    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type. We use Any here because
    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type is not supported by msgspec.
    logits_processors: Optional[Any] = None
    include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False
    truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int, msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None
    output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE

    # The below fields are not supposed to be used as an input.
    # They are set in post_init.
    output_text_buffer_length: int = 0
    _all_stop_token_ids: set[int] = msgspec.field(default_factory=set)

    # Fields used to construct logits processors
    guided_decoding: Optional[GuidedDecodingParams] = None
    logit_bias: Optional[dict[int, float]] = None
    allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
    extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None

    # Fields used for bad words
    bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None
    _bad_words_token_ids: Optional[list[list[int]]] = None

    @staticmethod
    def from_optional(
        n: Optional[int] = 1,
        best_of: Optional[int] = None,
        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = 0.0,
        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = 0.0,
        repetition_penalty: Optional[float] = 1.0,
        temperature: Optional[float] = 1.0,
        top_p: Optional[float] = 1.0,
        top_k: int = 0,
        min_p: float = 0.0,
        seed: Optional[int] = None,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None,
        stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None,
        bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None,
        include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False,
        ignore_eos: bool = False,
        max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16,
        min_tokens: int = 0,
        logprobs: Optional[int] = None,
        prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None,
        detokenize: bool = True,
        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
        logits_processors: Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] = None,
        truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int,
                                                   msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None,
        output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE,
        guided_decoding: Optional[GuidedDecodingParams] = None,
        logit_bias: Optional[Union[dict[int, float], dict[str, float]]] = None,
        allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None,
        extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
    ) -> "SamplingParams":
        if logit_bias is not None:
            # Convert token_id to integer
            # Clamp the bias between -100 and 100 per OpenAI API spec
            logit_bias = {
                int(token): min(100.0, max(-100.0, bias))
                for token, bias in logit_bias.items()
            }

        return SamplingParams(
            n=1 if n is None else n,
            best_of=best_of,
            presence_penalty=0.0
            if presence_penalty is None else presence_penalty,
            frequency_penalty=0.0
            if frequency_penalty is None else frequency_penalty,
            repetition_penalty=1.0
            if repetition_penalty is None else repetition_penalty,
            temperature=1.0 if temperature is None else temperature,
            top_p=1.0 if top_p is None else top_p,
            top_k=top_k,
            min_p=min_p,
            seed=seed,
            stop=stop,
            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
            bad_words=bad_words,
            include_stop_str_in_output=include_stop_str_in_output,
            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
            max_tokens=max_tokens,
            min_tokens=min_tokens,
            logprobs=logprobs,
            prompt_logprobs=prompt_logprobs,
            detokenize=detokenize,
            skip_special_tokens=skip_special_tokens,
            spaces_between_special_tokens=spaces_between_special_tokens,
            logits_processors=logits_processors,
            truncate_prompt_tokens=truncate_prompt_tokens,
            output_kind=output_kind,
            guided_decoding=guided_decoding,
            logit_bias=logit_bias,
            allowed_token_ids=allowed_token_ids,
            extra_args=extra_args,
        )

    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
        # how we deal with `best_of``:
        # if `best_of`` is not set, we default to `n`;
        # if `best_of`` is set, we set `n`` to `best_of`,
        # and set `_real_n`` to the original `n`.
        # when we return the result, we will check
        # if we need to return `n` or `_real_n` results
        if self.best_of:
            if self.best_of < self.n:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"best_of must be greater than or equal to n, "
                    f"got n={self.n} and best_of={self.best_of}.")
            if not self._real_n:
                self._real_n = self.n
                self.n = self.best_of

        if 0 < self.temperature < _MAX_TEMP:
            logger.warning(
                "temperature %s is less than %s, which may cause numerical "
                "errors nan or inf in tensors. We have maxed it out to %s.",
                self.temperature, _MAX_TEMP, _MAX_TEMP)
            self.temperature = max(self.temperature, _MAX_TEMP)

        if self.seed == -1:
            self.seed = None

        if self.stop is None:
            self.stop = []
        elif isinstance(self.stop, str):
            self.stop = [self.stop]

        if self.stop_token_ids is None:
            self.stop_token_ids = []

        if self.bad_words is None:
            self.bad_words = []

        if self.logprobs is True:
            self.logprobs = 1

        if self.prompt_logprobs is True:
            self.prompt_logprobs = 1

        # Number of characters to hold back for stop string evaluation
        # until sequence is finished.
        if self.stop and not self.include_stop_str_in_output:
            self.output_text_buffer_length = max(len(s) for s in self.stop) - 1

        self._verify_args()

        if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
            # Zero temperature means greedy sampling.
            self.top_p = 1.0
            self.top_k = 0
            self.min_p = 0.0
            self._verify_greedy_sampling()

        # eos_token_id is added to this by the engine
        self._all_stop_token_ids.update(self.stop_token_ids)

    def _verify_args(self) -> None:
        if not isinstance(self.n, int):
            raise ValueError(f"n must be an int, but is of "
                             f"type {type(self.n)}")
        if self.n < 1:
            raise ValueError(f"n must be at least 1, got {self.n}.")
        if self.best_of is not None:
            if not isinstance(self.best_of, int):
                raise ValueError(
                    f"best_of must be an integer, got {type(self.best_of)}")
            if self.best_of < 1:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"best_of must be at least 1, got {self.best_of}")
            if self.best_of < self.n:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"best_of must be greater than or equal to n, "
                    f"got n={self.n} and best_of={self.best_of}.")
        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError("presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
                             f"{self.presence_penalty}.")
        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError("frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
                             f"{self.frequency_penalty}.")
        if self.repetition_penalty <= 0.0:
            raise ValueError(
                "repetition_penalty must be greater than zero, got "
                f"{self.repetition_penalty}.")
        if self.temperature < 0.0:
            raise ValueError(
                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}.")
        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
        # quietly accept -1 as disabled, but prefer 0
        if self.top_k < -1:
            raise ValueError(f"top_k must be 0 (disable), or at least 1, "
                             f"got {self.top_k}.")
        if not isinstance(self.top_k, int):
            raise TypeError(
                f"top_k must be an integer, got {type(self.top_k).__name__}")
        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
            raise ValueError("min_p must be in [0, 1], got "
                             f"{self.min_p}.")
        if self.max_tokens is not None and self.max_tokens < 1:
            raise ValueError(
                f"max_tokens must be at least 1, got {self.max_tokens}.")
        if self.min_tokens < 0:
            raise ValueError(f"min_tokens must be greater than or equal to 0, "
                             f"got {self.min_tokens}.")
        if self.max_tokens is not None and self.min_tokens > self.max_tokens:
            raise ValueError(
                f"min_tokens must be less than or equal to "
                f"max_tokens={self.max_tokens}, got {self.min_tokens}.")
        if self.logprobs is not None and self.logprobs < 0:
            raise ValueError(
                f"logprobs must be non-negative, got {self.logprobs}.")
        if self.prompt_logprobs is not None and self.prompt_logprobs < 0:
            raise ValueError(f"prompt_logprobs must be non-negative, got "
                             f"{self.prompt_logprobs}.")
        if (self.truncate_prompt_tokens is not None
                and self.truncate_prompt_tokens < 1):
            raise ValueError(f"truncate_prompt_tokens must be >= 1, "
                             f"got {self.truncate_prompt_tokens}")
        assert isinstance(self.stop_token_ids, list)
        if not all(isinstance(st_id, int) for st_id in self.stop_token_ids):
            raise ValueError(f"stop_token_ids must contain only integers, "
                             f"got {self.stop_token_ids}.")
        assert isinstance(self.stop, list)
        if any(not stop_str for stop_str in self.stop):
            raise ValueError("stop cannot contain an empty string.")
        if self.stop and not self.detokenize:
            raise ValueError(
                "stop strings are only supported when detokenize is True. "
                "Set detokenize=True to use stop.")
        if self.best_of != self._real_n and self.output_kind == (
                RequestOutputKind.DELTA):
            raise ValueError("best_of must equal n to use output_kind=DELTA")

    def _verify_greedy_sampling(self) -> None:
        if self.n > 1:
            raise ValueError("n must be 1 when using greedy sampling, "
                             f"got {self.n}.")

    def update_from_generation_config(
            self,
            generation_config: dict[str, Any],
            model_eos_token_id: Optional[int] = None) -> None:
        """Update if there are non-default values from generation_config"""

        if model_eos_token_id is not None:
            # Add the eos token id into the sampling_params to support
            # min_tokens processing.
            self._all_stop_token_ids.add(model_eos_token_id)

        # Update eos_token_id for generation
        if (eos_ids := generation_config.get("eos_token_id")) is not None:
            # it can be either int or list of int
            eos_ids = {eos_ids} if isinstance(eos_ids, int) else set(eos_ids)
            if model_eos_token_id is not None:
                # We don't need to include the primary eos_token_id in
                # stop_token_ids since it's handled separately for stopping
                # purposes.
                eos_ids.discard(model_eos_token_id)
            if eos_ids:
                self._all_stop_token_ids.update(eos_ids)
                if not self.ignore_eos:
                    eos_ids.update(self.stop_token_ids)
                    self.stop_token_ids = list(eos_ids)

    def update_from_tokenizer(self, tokenizer: AnyTokenizer) -> None:
        if not self.bad_words:
            return
        self._bad_words_token_ids = []
        for bad_word in self.bad_words:
            # To prohibit words both at the beginning
            # and in the middle of text
            # (related to add_prefix_space tokenizer parameter)
            for add_prefix_space in [False, True]:
                prefix = " " if add_prefix_space else ""
                prompt = prefix + bad_word.lstrip()
                prompt_token_ids = tokenizer.encode(text=prompt,
                                                    add_special_tokens=False)

                # If no space at the beginning
                # or if prefix space produces a new word token
                if (not add_prefix_space) or (
                        add_prefix_space and prompt_token_ids[0]
                        != self._bad_words_token_ids[-1][0]
                        and len(prompt_token_ids) == len(
                            self._bad_words_token_ids[-1])):
                    self._bad_words_token_ids.append(prompt_token_ids)

        invalid_token_ids = [
            token_id for bad_words_token_ids in self._bad_words_token_ids
            for token_id in bad_words_token_ids
            if token_id < 0 or token_id > tokenizer.max_token_id
        ]
        if len(invalid_token_ids) > 0:
            raise ValueError(
                f"The model vocabulary size is {tokenizer.max_token_id+1},"
                f" but the following tokens"
                f" were specified as bad: {invalid_token_ids}."
                f" All token id values should be integers satisfying:"
                f" 0 <= token_id <= {tokenizer.max_token_id}.")

    @cached_property
    def sampling_type(self) -> SamplingType:
        if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
            return SamplingType.GREEDY
        if self.seed is not None:
            return SamplingType.RANDOM_SEED
        return SamplingType.RANDOM

    @property
    def all_stop_token_ids(self) -> set[int]:
        return self._all_stop_token_ids

    @property
    def bad_words_token_ids(self) -> Optional[list[list[int]]]:
        # For internal use only. Backward compatibility not guaranteed
        return self._bad_words_token_ids

    def clone(self) -> "SamplingParams":
        """Deep copy, but maybe not the LogitsProcessor objects.

        LogitsProcessor objects may contain an arbitrary, nontrivial amount of
        data that is expensive to copy. However, if not copied, the processor
        needs to support parallel decoding for multiple sequences
        See https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/3087
        """

        logit_processor_refs = None if self.logits_processors is None else {
            id(lp): lp.clone() if hasattr(lp, 'clone') else lp
            for lp in self.logits_processors
        }
        return copy.deepcopy(self, memo=logit_processor_refs)

    def __repr__(self) -> str:
        return (
            f"SamplingParams(n={self.n}, "
            f"presence_penalty={self.presence_penalty}, "
            f"frequency_penalty={self.frequency_penalty}, "
            f"repetition_penalty={self.repetition_penalty}, "
            f"temperature={self.temperature}, "
            f"top_p={self.top_p}, "
            f"top_k={self.top_k}, "
            f"min_p={self.min_p}, "
            f"seed={self.seed}, "
            f"stop={self.stop}, "
            f"stop_token_ids={self.stop_token_ids}, "
            f"bad_words={self.bad_words}, "
            f"include_stop_str_in_output={self.include_stop_str_in_output}, "
            f"ignore_eos={self.ignore_eos}, "
            f"max_tokens={self.max_tokens}, "
            f"min_tokens={self.min_tokens}, "
            f"logprobs={self.logprobs}, "
            f"prompt_logprobs={self.prompt_logprobs}, "
            f"skip_special_tokens={self.skip_special_tokens}, "
            "spaces_between_special_tokens="
            f"{self.spaces_between_special_tokens}, "
            f"truncate_prompt_tokens={self.truncate_prompt_tokens}, "
            f"guided_decoding={self.guided_decoding}, "
            f"extra_args={self.extra_args})")


class BeamSearchParams(
        msgspec.Struct,
        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
        # required for @cached_property.
        dict=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
    """Beam search parameters for text generation."""
    beam_width: int
    max_tokens: int
    ignore_eos: bool = False
    temperature: float = 0.0
    length_penalty: float = 1.0
    include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False
